"cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-94", "name": "Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-843", "name": "Access of Resource Using Incompatible Type ('Type Confusion')"}], "description": "### Impact

Using Babel to compile code that was specifically crafted by an attacker can cause Babel to generate output code that executes arbitrary code.

Known affected plugins are:
- `@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs`
- `@babel/preset-env` when using the [`modules: "systemjs"` option](https://babel.dev/docs/babel-preset-env#modules), as it delegates to `@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs`

No other plugins under the `@babel` namespace are impacted.

**Users that only compile trusted code are not impacted.**

### Patches

The vulnerability has been fixed in `@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs@7.29.4`.

Babel also released `@babel/preset-env@7.29.5`, updating its `@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs` dependency, to simplify forcing the update if you are using `@babel/preset-env` directly.

### Workarounds

- Pin `@babel/parser` to v7.11.5. The downgrade will completely disable string module name parsing, but it would also disable other new language features and the build pipeline may fail as a result. Only do so if you are working on a legacy codebase and can not upgrade `@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs` to v7.29.4.
- Do not use the `modules: "systemjs"` option, migrate the codebase to native ES Modules or any other module formats.

### Credits
Babel thanks Daniel Cervera for reporting the vulnerability.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00125, "percentile": 0.02532}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-fv7c-fp4j-7gwp", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-08T20:34:07Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fv7c-fp4j-7gwp", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-fv7c-fp4j-7gwp"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44728"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-26T18:16:50Z", "published_at": "2026-05-08T20:34:07Z", "references": ["https://github.com/babel/babel/security/advisories/GHSA-fv7c-fp4j-7gwp", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44728", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fv7c-fp4j-7gwp"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/babel/babel/security-advisories/GHSA-fv7c-fp4j-7gwp", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/babel/babel", "summary": "@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs generates arbitrary code when compiling malicious input", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-08T23:28:10Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-fv7c-fp4j-7gwp", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "7.29.4", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 7.12.0, <= 7.29.3"}, {"first_patched_version": "8.0.0-alpha.13", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 8.0.0-alpha.0, <= 8.0.0-alpha.12"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: @babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs generates arbitrary code when compiling malicious input
